questions.text: An inertial frame of reference is one which satisfies:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: An inertial frame of reference is one that satisfies the following conditions: It is not accelerating. It is not rotating. It is not experiencing any external forces. In an inertial frame of reference, Newton's laws of motion hold true. This means that if an object is at rest in an inertial frame of reference, it will remain at rest unless acted upon by a force. Similarly, if an object is moving at a constant velocity in an inertial frame of reference, it will continue to move at that velocity unless acted upon by a force.
Einstein's theory is a broad term that can refer to any of Einstein's theories, but it is most commonly used to refer to his theory of special relativity.

Hertz theory is a theory of electromagnetism that was developed by Heinrich Hertz in the late 19th century. It states that electromagnetic waves are propagated through space as transverse waves and that they travel at the speed of light.

The special theory of relativity is a scientific theory of the relationship between space and time. It was developed by Albert Einstein in 1905, and it is based on two postulates: The laws of physics are the same in all inertial frames of reference (that is, frames of reference with no acceleration). The speed of light in a vacuum is the same for all observers, regardless of their relative motion or the motion of the light source.

questions.explanation: An inertial frame of reference is one that satisfies the following conditions: It is not accelerating. It is not rotating. It is not experiencing any external forces. In an inertial frame of reference, Newton's laws of motion hold true. This means that if an object is at rest in an inertial frame of reference, it will remain at rest unless acted upon by a force. Similarly, if an object is moving at a constant velocity in an inertial frame of reference, it will continue to move at that velocity unless acted upon by a force.

questions.text: A non-inertial frame of reference has:

options.explanation: It is not consistent with the definition of a non-inertial frame of reference.
It is not consistent with the definition of a non-inertial frame of reference.
It is not consistent with the definition of a non-inertial frame of reference.

A non-inertial frame of reference is a frame of reference that is accelerating, either in linear fashion or rotating around some axis. This means that the frame of reference is not at rest or moving at a constant velocity.

questions.text: Which one of the following statements is incorrect in perspective to modern physics?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This statement is correct. According to Einstein's theory of relativity, energy exhibits inertia, which is a fundamental concept in modern physics.

options.text: Time dilation phenomenon does not occur in daily life.
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Although time dilation is a real effect predicted by relativity, it is not noticeable in our daily experiences due to the relatively slow speeds and weak gravitational fields we encounter.
This statement is correct. Light is indeed a form of electromagnetic radiation, which is a well-established fact in modern physics.

This statement is incorrect. Current observations in modern physics suggest that the universe is expanding, not contracting, which aligns with the Big Bang theory and the discovery of cosmic microwave background radiation.

questions.explanation: The statement 'The universe is continuously contracting' is incorrect because current scientific evidence supports the idea that the universe is expanding. This expansion is supported by observations such as the redshift of galaxies and the cosmic microwave background radiation. In contrast, the other options reflect accurate concepts within the framework of modern physics. Option A is correct as energy does have inertia, as described by Einstein's mass-energy equivalence. Option B is correct because while time dilation is a real effect, it doesn't significantly impact our everyday experiences. Option C is also correct, as light is a well-known form of electromagnetic radiation.

questions.text: The rest mass energy of the electron is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The rest mass energy of an electron can be calculated using Einstein's equation:E = mc²E = 9.11 × 10-31 × (3 × 108)²E = 9.11 × 10-31 × 9 × 1016E = 9.11 × 9 × 10-15E = 8.19 × 10-14 JE ≈ = 8.2 × 10-14 JSo, the rest mass energy of the electron is approximately 8.2 × 10-14 Joule.

questions.text: In Einstein's universe, what is the fourth dimension?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: While distance is a fundamental quantity in three-dimensional space, it is not considered the fourth dimension in Einstein's universe.
Speed is a measure of how fast an object is moving and is not considered a dimension in the context of Einstein's universe.

Explanation: In Einstein's theory of relativity, spacetime is a four-dimensional continuum that combines the three dimensions of space with the fourth dimension of time. This concept of spacetime is fundamental to understanding the relativistic nature of space, time, and motion.
Energy is a scalar quantity that represents the ability of a system to do work and is not considered a dimension in the context of Einstein's universe.

questions.explanation: Explanation: In Einstein's theory of relativity, spacetime is a four-dimensional continuum that combines the three dimensions of space with the fourth dimension of time. This concept of spacetime is fundamental to understanding the relativistic nature of space, time, and motion.

The answer maximum is wrong because there is no maximum speed for light.

options.explanation: The answer remain the same is wrong because the length of an object is contracted along the direction of motion when it is moving at relativistic speeds.

The relativistic length of a moving object will be decreased, as seen by an observer moving relative to the object. This is a consequence of the special theory of relativity, which states that the length of an object is contracted along the direction of motion when it is moving at relativistic speeds.
The answer increased is wrong because the length of an object cannot be increased by motion.
The answer doubled is wrong because the length of an object is not doubled by motion.

questions.text: If the rest mass of a particle is zero, then its speed is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If the rest mass of a particle is zero, then its speed is speed of light. This is because the rest mass of a particle is a measure of the inertia of the particle, and inertia is a measure of how much resistance a particle has to change its motion. A particle with zero rest mass has no inertia, and therefore it can move at the speed of light.

options.text: Less than the speed of light
options.explanation: The answer less than the speed of light is wrong because a particle with zero rest mass cannot move at a speed less than the speed of light.

options.text: Greater than the speed of light
options.explanation: The answer more than the speed of light is wrong because the speed of light is the maximum speed that any particle can travel.

options.text: Not comparable with speed of light
options.explanation: The answer not comparable to the speed of light is wrong because the speed of light is a well-defined speed, and it is perfectly comparable to the speed of a particle with zero rest mass.

questions.explanation: If the rest mass of a particle is zero, then its speed is the speed of light. This is because the rest mass of a particle is a measure of the inertia of the particle, and inertia is a measure of how much resistance a particle has to change its motion. A particle with zero rest mass has no inertia, and therefore it can move at the speed of light.

questions.text: In special theory of relativity, the law of conservation of energy and law of conservation of mass have been replaced with
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: The law of conservation of mass only
options.explanation: This is also not correct, as mass and energy are interchangeable, and mass is also conserved.This is not correct, as mass and energy are interchangeable in special relativity

In the special theory of relativity, Albert Einstein showed that mass (m) and energy (E) are equivalent and can be converted into each other, as expressed by the famous equation:E = mc^2This means that the laws of conservation of energy and conservation of mass are no longer separate, but are combined into a single law: the law of conservation of mass-energy. This law states that the total mass-energy of a closed system remains constant over time.

options.text: law of conservation of energy only
options.explanation: This is also not correct, as mass and energy are interchangeable, and mass is also conserved.This is not correct, as mass and energy are interchangeable in special relativity.
This is not correct, as only the law of conservation of mass-energy is the correct replacement

questions.explanation: In the special theory of relativity, Albert Einstein showed that mass (m) and energy (E) are equivalent and can be converted into each other, as expressed by the famous equation:E = mc^2This means that the laws of conservation of energy and conservation of mass are no longer separate, but are combined into a single law: the law of conservation of mass-energy. This law states that the total mass-energy of a closed system remains constant over time.

questions.text: The keynote of Einstein's theory of relativity is that gravity is a property of
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Length-contraction is a concept in special relativity that describes how lengths can appear shorter when observed from a moving reference frame relative to a stationary frame. While it is a consequence of relativity, it is not the fundamental property that describes gravity.

This option is incorrect. The space-time interval refers to the concept in special relativity that combines both space and time dimensions into a unified framework. It describes the separation between two events in space and time. While it is an important concept in relativity, it does not directly address the nature of gravity.

This option is correct. The correct answer is c. Space-time geometry. Einstein's theory of relativity, both special relativity and general relativity, fundamentally altered our understanding of gravity. According to general relativity, gravity is not a force but a curvature of space-time caused by the presence of mass and energy. In this theory, the motion of objects is influenced by the curvature of space-time, resulting in the effects we observe as gravity.

This option is incorrect. Mass-energy equivalence, famously described by Einstein's equation E=mc², states that mass and energy are interchangeable and are different forms of the same underlying concept. While it is a significant concept in relativity, it does not directly address the nature of gravity.

questions.explanation: Einstein's theory of relativity transformed the understanding of gravity from a force acting at a distance, as in Newtonian physics, to an effect of the curvature of space-time. This curvature is created by mass and energy, which alter the geometry of space-time itself, causing objects to follow curved paths. This is why the correct answer is space-time geometry. The other options—length-contraction, space-time interval, and mass-energy equivalence—are important concepts in relativity but do not specifically describe gravity as space-time geometry does.

questions.text: According to the special theory of relativity, mass of a moving object is considered as infinite when it moves with
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A suggests that an object's mass becomes infinite when it moves with the velocity of light. This option is correct.

Option B suggests that an object's mass becomes infinite when it moves with half the velocity of light. This is not consistent with the theory of relativity. As an object's velocity increases, its mass increases, but it remains finite as long as it is below the speed of light. Therefore, option B is incorrect.

Option C suggests that an object's mass becomes infinite when it moves with twice the velocity of light. However, velocities greater than the speed of light are not allowed in the theory of relativity. The speed of light is an absolute limit, and it is not possible for an object to exceed it. Therefore, option C is incorrect.
Object's mass will not become infinite when it moves with thrice the velocity of light. So, it is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The special theory of relativity postulates that as an object with mass accelerates towards the speed of light, its relativistic mass increases asymptotically, effectively becoming infinite at the speed of light. This is due to the relativistic effects that occur at velocities approaching the speed of light. As a result, objects with mass cannot reach or exceed the speed of light, making it an absolute cosmic speed limit. Consequently, options B, C, and D are incorrect as they are either below or exceed this fundamental speed limit.

questions.text: Objects cannot be accelerated to the speed of light in free space is consequence of
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: a) Mass variation: This option refers to the concept of mass increasing as an object approaches the speed of light. According to Einstein's theory of relativity, as an object's speed approaches the speed of light, its relativistic mass increases. This means that the amount of force required to continue accelerating the object also increases, making it increasingly difficult to accelerate it further. This effect becomes more significant as the speed of the object gets closer to the speed of light.

b) Energy - mass relationship: This option is related to Einstein's famous equation, E=mc², which states that energy (E) is equal to mass (m) times the speed of light (c) squared. This equation shows the equivalence of mass and energy. When an object approaches the speed of light, its kinetic energy increases dramatically, and this energy increase contributes to the object's increased mass. As a result, more and more energy is required to accelerate the object, making it impossible to reach the speed of light.

c) Inertia forces: Inertia refers to an object's resistance to changes in its state of motion. As an object's speed approaches the speed of light, its inertia becomes infinitely large. This means that the force required to accelerate the object further also becomes infinitely large, making it physically impossible to reach or exceed the speed of light.

d) All of these: This option is correct. All three factors mentioned above contribute to the impossibility of accelerating an object with mass to the speed of light. The mass variation, energy-mass relationship, and the increase in inertia forces all work together to prevent objects with mass from achieving or exceeding the speed of light in free space.

questions.explanation: In this case, we look at mass alteration as the speed of object increases.The mathematical expression can be given as, m=mo/√(1-v2/ c2)If v2 = c2Then, m=mo/√(1-c2/ c2),m=mo/√(1-1)m=mo/0m=∞Since the mass would increase to infinity, the energy required to accelerate it would be infinity which is not possible.

questions.text: In Einstein‘s universe what is the fourth dimension:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Distance is one of the three spatial dimensions that contribute to the structure of space. In Einstein's framework of spacetime, distance is part of the three spatial dimensions, but it is not the fourth dimension.

Speed measures the rate of motion through space but is not a dimension itself. In Einstein's theories, speed affects time perception, yet it does not constitute a separate dimension in the spacetime continuum.

Time is the fourth dimension in Einstein's theory of relativity. It integrates with the three spatial dimensions to form a four-dimensional spacetime continuum, fundamentally altering our understanding of the universe.
Energy is a vital concept in physics, related to mass by Einstein's E=mc² equation. While significant to his theories, energy itself is not a dimension in the spacetime model.

questions.explanation: Einstein's theory of relativity introduced the revolutionary concept of spacetime, where the three dimensions of space are intertwined with the dimension of time, forming a four-dimensional continuum. This framework allows for the description of events based on four coordinates: three for space and one for time. Time, as the fourth dimension, is integral to understanding how objects move and interact in the universe, particularly under the influence of gravity.The incorrect options can be explained as follows: Distance and speed are related to spatial movement, but they do not represent dimensions in spacetime. Energy, while crucial in Einstein's theories, is not a dimension but rather a quantity that links mass through relativity.

The proper time interval between two events is the amount of elapsed time reckoned on the clock of an observer in an inertial frame of reference who is physically present at both events. So if the observer needs to be present at both events, they must take place at the same coordinates.

questions.text: Identify the postulate/s of the special theory of relativity:I. There is no preferred or absolute inertial frame of reference i.e. all the interstitial frames are equivalent for the description of all physical law.II. The speed of light in a vacuum is the same for all observers in uniform translational relative motion and is independent of the motion of the observer and the source.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: While statement I is correct, it is not the only postulate of the special theory of relativity. Make sure to consider all the key principles.
Statement II is true, but it is not the only principle that underpins the theory. Review the complete set of postulates.
Both statements are correct and together they form the foundation of Einstein's special theory of relativity.
This option is incorrect because both statements are indeed the core postulates of the special theory of relativity.

questions.explanation: The special theory of relativity is based on two fundamental postulates proposed by Albert Einstein. The first postulate states that there is no preferred or absolute inertial frame of reference, meaning that the laws of physics are the same in all inertial frames. This implies that all inertial frames are equivalent for the formulation of physical laws. The second postulate asserts that the speed of light in a vacuum is constant and is the same for all observers, regardless of the motion of the light source or the observer. Therefore, both statements I and II are correct. Option D is incorrect because both postulates are indeed valid and integral to the theory.

questions.text: What is the mass "m" of a moving object with speed 0.8 c.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Correct. This option uses the relativistic mass formula correctly with the given speed.
Incorrect. This option does not correctly apply the relativistic mass formula.
Incorrect. This option overestimates the effect of relativistic speed on mass.
Incorrect. This option significantly overestimates the relativistic mass.
Incorrect. This option greatly exaggerates the mass increase due to relativistic effects.

questions.explanation: The relativistic mass m of an object moving at a speed v is given by the formula: m = m0 / sqrt(1 - v2/c2) where m0 is the rest mass and c is the speed of light. For v = 0.8c, the mass becomes: m = m0 / sqrt(1 - (0.8c)2/c2) = m0 / sqrt(1 - 0.64) = m0 / sqrt(0.36) = m0 / 0.6 = 1.67m0. Options B, C, D, and E all incorrectly compute the relativistic mass, either by miscalculating the effect of velocity on mass or by using an incorrect formula.

questions.text: The special theory of relativity is based upon I) The laws of physics are same in all inertial frame .II) The speed of light in free space has the same value for all observers, regardless of their state of motion.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A: Incorrect because it includes only statement I, which is incomplete. The special theory of relativity is based on both statements I and II.
Option B: Incorrect because it includes only statement II, which is also incomplete. The special theory of relativity is based on both statements I and II.

The correct option is C: I and II only. The special theory of relativity is indeed based on both statements, which are as follows:I) The laws of physics are the same in all inertial frames (frames moving at constant velocity relative to each other).II) The speed of light in a vacuum has the same value (about 299,792,458 meters per second) for all observers, regardless of their state of motion.
Incorrect as option C is correct

questions.explanation: Einstein's special theory of relativity states that the same laws of physics hold true in all inertial reference frames and that the speed of light is the same for all observers, even those moving with respect to one another

questions.text: Special theory of relativity solves problems with?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The special theory of relativity addresses problems within inertial frames of reference, where objects move at constant velocity and are not subject to external forces.
Accelerating frames involve non-inertial motion, which is not covered by the special theory of relativity; instead, this is the realm of general relativity.
While both inertial and accelerating frames are important in physics, the special theory of relativity specifically handles inertial frames, leaving accelerating frames to general relativity.
The special theory of relativity is indeed applicable to inertial frames, so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The special theory of relativity, proposed by Albert Einstein, applies primarily to inertial frames of reference—those in which objects are either at rest or move at a constant velocity. It revolutionizes concepts of space and time, particularly when dealing with objects moving at high speeds close to the speed of light. In contrast, the general theory of relativity addresses accelerating frames of reference and the effects of gravity. Therefore, the correct answer is that the special theory of relativity deals with inertial frames. Options B and C are incorrect because they involve accelerating frames, which are not covered by the special theory. Option D is incorrect as the theory is indeed applicable to inertial frames.

questions.text: Which photon is traveling with the largest speed in a vacuum?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Gamma photons are high-energy electromagnetic waves with very short wavelengths. They are emitted during gamma decay, which is a nuclear decay process. Gamma photons, like all photons, travel at the speed of light in a vacuum.

Visible light photons are the photons that our eyes can detect, and they are responsible for the colors we see. Visible light has a range of wavelengths, with violet light having the shortest wavelength and red light having the longest. Just like all other photons, visible light photons travel at the speed of light in a vacuum.

Infrared photons have longer wavelengths than visible light and are associated with heat radiation. They are commonly used in infrared imaging and heating applications. Similar to gamma and visible light photons, infrared photons travel at the speed of light in a vacuum.

options.text: All photons move with the speed of light
options.explanation: All photons move with the speed of light - This statement is true. In a vacuum, all photons, regardless of their energy or wavelength, travel at the speed of light, denoted by the letter "c." The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792 kilometers per second (km/s) or about 186,282 miles per second (mi/s).

questions.explanation: A vacuum refers to the space in which no matter is present. Therefore, any object moving inside such space faces no frictional forces and so will naturally move faster.The reason here is that in a vacuum, the photons of any EM wave travel at equal speeds. According to this formula, the product of frequency and wavelength remains constant for a given photon which is the speed of light.c= fλc=3.00 × 108 m/sc=3.00 × 108 m/sRegardless of frequency or wavelength (which is what distinguishes each option from the other), the speed of the photon will be the same. However, the propagation speed through anything with a refractive index ( or optical density ) is slower than this. Generally speaking, longer wavelengths travel through some mediums faster than others.

questions.text: As compared to a stationary clock, a moving dock clicks: rate as stationary clicks
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: According to Einstein's Special Theory of Relativity, time for a moving reference frame actually slows down compared to a stationary observer. This phenomenon is known as time dilation.
This is incorrect. According to relativity, time does not speed up in a moving reference frame; it slows down.
This is incorrect. If time were the same, it would imply no effect of relativity, which contradicts the well-established principles of the theory.
This is incorrect and is a nonsensical option in the context of relativity, as time cannot be negative.

questions.explanation: According to Einstein’s Special Theory of Relativity, Time for a moving reference frame actually slows down compared to a “stationary” observer. This works in principle no matter what kind of clock is used, e.g., a wrist watch, crystal oscillator, atomic clock, an hourglass filled with sand, or even a clock made from bouncing light in a mirror setup (which served as Einstein’s original thought experiment). All of this is possible because time itself slows, regardless of how it is measured. According to Einstein’s theory, the amount that time slows is more pronounced the faster an object travels through space (especially as its velocity gets closer to the speed of light); however, at typical speeds encountered in our everyday lives, there is essentially no change in time due to Relativity. For clocks that move at a constant speed, the time change (time dilation) is constant, whereas when clocks are being accelerated (whether linearly or in a gravitational field), the amount of time dilation is governed by Einstein’s General Theory of Relativity. Both Einstein’s Special Theory of Relativity and General Theory of Relativity have been confirmed in every single experiment performed (within the margins of error), except they aren’t compatible with Quantum Mechanics.

questions.text: To an observer stationary on a plateform a compared to a stationary clock and a moving clock clicks:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: According to the theory of relativity, time appears to move slower for a moving clock as observed from a stationary frame of reference.
A moving clock cannot tick faster than a stationary clock relative to an observer, according to the principles of special relativity.
While this might seem intuitive, relativity tells us that the moving clock will tick slower, not at the same rate.
Time cannot click negatively; this option is not consistent with the principles of time measurement.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that the moving clock ticks slower as observed from a stationary platform. This phenomenon is explained by the theory of relativity, specifically time dilation, which states that a clock moving relative to an observer will measure time more slowly than a clock at rest with respect to that observer. Option B is incorrect because relativity does not allow for a moving clock to tick faster. Option C is incorrect because it contradicts the experimental and theoretical background of time dilation. Option D is incorrect because time cannot be negative, as time is always a positive measure moving forward.

questions.text: A meter rod is moving with speed of light with respect to a stationary observer. The length of the rod will appear to the observer as approaching:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Formula for length contraction is L=L0√1−v2/c2, where: • L = new length (m) • L0 = original length (m) • v = speed of object (ms−1) • c = speed of light (~3.00⋅108ms−1) So, L=√1−c2/c2 =√1−1 =√0 L=0 meter

questions.text: the energy of light is determined by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: False.The energy of light is indeed determined by its frequency. The frequency of light refers to the number of complete cycles or oscillations it makes per second. According to the wave-particle duality of light, light can be viewed as both a wave and a particle called a photon. The energy of a single photon is directly proportional to its frequency. In other words, higher frequency light carries more energy per photon than lower frequency light.

questions.text: In Einstein's universe what is the fourth dimension?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: False!In Einstein's universe, time is considered the fourth dimension. In addition to the three spatial dimensions (length, width, and height), Einstein's theory of relativity introduced the concept of time as a fourth dimension. This unified spacetime, where both space and time are interconnected. It means that an event's position in spacetime requires four coordinates: three for space and one for time. This understanding of spacetime helps explain how time can be affected by gravity and motion.

questions.text: If the clouds of the Earth behave like a black body, what will happen?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: No light will reach the surface
options.explanation: Light is also a form of radiation. Hence, the clouds will absorb all light. As a result, no light will reach the surface, and this option is correct.

options.text: Extra light will reach the surface
options.explanation: No light will reach the surface, so ‘extra light’ is out of the question. Hence, this option is incorrect.
As all the light will get absorbed and no light will reach the surface, we cannot say that nothing will happen. Hence, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Some light will reflect, and some will be absorbed
options.explanation: Theoretically, a black body does not reflect light; hence, the clouds will not reflect light. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: If Earth's clouds were perfect black bodies, then they would absorb the sunlight falling on them and radiate that energy as heat. The heat would reach the surface of the Earth and the oceans, causing more clouds to form by the process of evaporation, followed by condensation. More clouds would eventually result in no light reaching the Earth's surface.

questions.text: The radiation emitted by hot bodies have color:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A hot body emits radiations, having perceived wavelengths changing from infrared to red, and then from red to orange, and so on. So, the option, closest to the answer, is Option A.

questions.text: A body at temperature T radiates heat according to relation:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is not the correct relation for the radiation of heat by a body at temperature T. The correct relation is related to Stefan-Boltzmann's Law, which states that the total radiant power (heat) emitted by a black body per unit area is proportional to the fourth power of its absolute temperature (T). So, the correct relation is T4.

By the Stefan-Boltzmann Law, for a black body, the amount of heat radiated per unit area is directly proportional to the fourth power of the kelvin temperature. The proportionality constant is called Stefen’s constant and has a value of 5.67x10(-8).Option A,C and D are incorrect
This option is not the correct relation for the radiation of heat by a body at temperature T. The correct relation is T4 as stated in option (b).
This option suggests that none of the previous options is correct. However, option (b) - T4 - is the correct relation for the radiation of heat by a body at temperature T.

questions.explanation: By the Stefan-Boltzmann Law, for a black body, the amount of heat radiated per unit area is directly proportional to the fourth power of the kelvin temperature. The proportionality constant is called Stefen’s constant and has a value of 5.67x10(-8).

questions.text: Which of the following blocks will release heat fast?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This surface will reflect more light and absorb less, as it is white. Roughness might increase its surface area slightly, allowing for a bit more absorption, but overall, it will reflect a significant portion of incoming radiation. Reflecting light means less energy is absorbed, leading to slower heating compared to absorbing it.

A polished white surface will reflect even more light than a rough white surface. Polishing a surface makes it smoother, which enhances its reflective properties. Therefore, this surface will absorb even less heat compared to the rough white surface and release heat more slowly.

A rough black surface will absorb more light and heat compared to the other surfaces listed. Black surfaces absorb more light across a broader spectrum, and the roughness can further enhance this absorption by trapping light within the surface irregularities. Consequently, this surface will release heat faster as it absorbs more energy.

Although black surfaces absorb more light and heat compared to white surfaces, polishing a black surface will make it more reflective. As a result, it will absorb less heat compared to a rough black surface and release heat slower.

questions.explanation: The key factor in determining how fast a surface releases heat is its ability to absorb light. Surfaces that absorb more light will heat up faster and consequently release heat faster.Black surfaces absorb more light compared to white surfaces because they absorb a broader spectrum of light. Therefore, a black surface, whether rough or polished, will generally release heat faster than a white surface.Roughness can enhance absorption to some extent by increasing the surface area for light to interact with. However, the color of the surface remains a dominant factor in determining its ability to absorb light.Polishing a surface generally increases its reflectivity, regardless of its color. So, polished surfaces, whether white or black, will absorb less light compared to their rough counterparts, leading to slower heat release.

questions.text: At high temperature, the proportion of _ wavelength radiation increases.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: a) AM radio: AM stands for "Amplitude Modulation," and AM radio stations typically transmit signals in the medium-frequency (MF) range. These signals are characterized by changes in the amplitude (height) of the radio waves. The proportion of AM radio wavelength radiation does not necessarily increase at high temperatures.

b) Long radio: This option seems to refer to longer wavelengths in the radio frequency spectrum. Long radio waves are typically associated with low-frequency (LF) and very low-frequency (VLF) signals. The proportion of long radio wavelength radiation might increase at high temperatures due to various atmospheric effects.

c) Shorter: As the temperature increases, the atoms and molecules in a material vibrate more vigorously, which can lead to the emission of higher-energy photons. Higher-energy photons are associated with shorter wavelengths according to the electromagnetic spectrum. So, the proportion of shorter wavelength radiation generally increases at high temperatures.

d) Both A and C: This option suggests that both AM radio wavelength radiation (Option A) and shorter wavelength radiation (Option C) increase at high temperatures. While Option A might not be accurate, Option C is correct.

questions.explanation: When the temperature of an object is increased it emits light of shorter wavelength according to E=hc/lambda, since the energy of the object increases the wavelength becomes short.

questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct value of Wien's constant, approximately 2.897 x 10-3 mK. It represents the product of the wavelength at maximum intensity and the absolute temperature of a blackbody.

questions.explanation: Wien's Law states that the product of the wavelength corresponding to the maximum intensity of radiation and the temperature of the body in Kelvin is a constant, known as Wien's constant. The correct value of this constant is approximately 2.897 x 10-3 mK. This relationship helps determine the peak wavelength of radiation emitted by a blackbody at any given temperature. Option A correctly reflects this value. The other options are incorrect as they suggest values that are either too small or not relevant to Wien's constant.

questions.text: If the temperature of the black body doubles, the intensity of radiation from it will become:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: BLACK BODY: A blackbody is a theoretical or model body that absorbs all radiation falling on it, reflecting or transmitting none. It is a hypothetical object that is a “perfect” absorber and “perfect” emitter of radiation over all wavelengths. Stefan–Boltzmann Law: It relates the total energy emitted (E) to the absolute temperature (T). E ∝ T⁴ We know that, intensity of radiation of black body is directly proportional to its energy. Thus, it can be concluded that if we increase the temperature of black body by 2 (i.e., we double it), the intensity increases by 2⁴ i.e. it increases 16 times). Thus, option ‘D’ is the correct answer.

questions.explanation: BLACK BODY: A blackbody is a theoretical or model body that absorbs all radiation falling on it, reflecting or transmitting none. It is a hypothetical object that is a “perfect” absorber and “perfect” emitter of radiation over all wavelengths. Stefan-Boltzmann Law: It relates the total energy emitted (E) to the absolute temperature (T). E ∝ T⁴ We know that the intensity of the radiation from a black body is directly proportional to its energy. Thus, it can be concluded that if we increase the temperature of the black body by 2 (i.e., we double it), the intensity increases by 2⁴ i.e. it increases 16 times). Thus, option ‘D’ is the correct answer.

questions.text: What is the rest mass of a photon?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Photons, being massless particles, have a rest mass of zero. According to special relativity, photons always travel at the speed of light (c) and have no rest mass. This means that a photon's energy (E) is equal to its momentum (p) multiplied by the speed of light (E = pc), and its rest mass is zero.

options.text: Equal to the mass of electron
options.explanation: The rest mass of an electron is approximately 9.11 × 10^-31 kilograms, which is not the same as the rest mass of a photon.

options.text: Equal to the mass of proton
options.explanation: The rest mass of a proton is approximately 1.67 × 10^-27 kilograms, which is also not the same as the rest mass of a photon
The rest mass of a photon is zero, not infinite

questions.explanation: Photons, being massless particles, have a rest mass of zero. According to special relativity, photons always travel at the speed of light (c) and have no rest mass. This means that a photon's energy (E) is equal to its momentum (p) multiplied by the speed of light (E = pc), and its rest mass is zero.

questions.text: Which light photon has the least momentum?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Red light has the longest wavelength of all visible light, about 700 nanometers. The momentum of a photon is inversely proportional to its wavelength, so the photon with the longest wavelength will have the least momentum. Therefore, the momentum of a red photon is the least of all visible light photons.
Green light has a wavelength of about 550 nanometers. This is shorter than the wavelength of red light, so the momentum of a green photon is greater than the momentum of a red photon.

Yellow light has a wavelength of about 580 nanometers. This is between the wavelengths of red and green light, so the momentum of a yellow photon is between the momentum of a red photon and the momentum of a green photon.
Blue light has a wavelength of about 400 nanometers. This is the shortest wavelength of all visible light, so the momentum of a blue photon is the greatest of all visible light photons.

questions.explanation: The light photon with the least momentum is red. This is because red light has the longest wavelength of all visible light. The momentum of a photon is inversely proportional to its wavelength, so the photon with the longest wavelength will have the least momentum.The momentum of a photon can be calculated using the following equation:p = h/λwhere p is the momentum, h is Planck's constant, and λ is the wavelength.The wavelength of red light is about 700 nanometers, while the wavelength of blue light is about 400 nanometers. This means that the momentum of a red photon is about half the momentum of a blue photon.Therefore, the light photon with the least momentum is red.

questions.text: Planck's constant was named after the scientist?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Einstein: Albert Einstein was a renowned physicist, but he is not directly associated with Planck's constant. However, Einstein made significant contributions to the field of theoretical physics, and he is well-known for his work on the photoelectric effect, which played a role in the development of quantum theory.

Maxwell: James Clerk Maxwell was a Scottish physicist who formulated the classical theory of electromagnetic fields. While Maxwell made crucial contributions to the understanding of electromagnetism, he is not directly linked to Planck's constant. His work laid the foundation for later developments in physics, including the theory of relativity and quantum mechanics.

Newton: Sir Isaac Newton was a mathematician and physicist who made foundational contributions to classical mechanics and the laws of motion. However, Newton's work predates the development of quantum mechanics, and he is not associated with Planck's constant.

Planck: Max Planck was a German physicist who is considered the father of quantum theory. In 1900, he introduced the concept of quantization of energy to explain the observed behavior of blackbody radiation. As part of his groundbreaking work, he introduced a fundamental constant that came to be known as Planck's constant (h). This constant is a crucial parameter in quantum mechanics and is named in honor of Max Planck for his pioneering contributions to the field.

questions.explanation: The Planck constant (Planck's constant) tells us what would be the change in the energy of a photon when the frequency of its electromagnetic wave increases by 1 (In SI Units). It is named after the physicist Max Planck.In SI Units the Planck constant is exactly 6.626×10-34 J·s.

questions.text: Planck's constant is analogous to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This Option is incorrect because the moment of inertia is not analogous to mass in linear momentum.

This Option is incorrect because wave number is analogous to temporal frequency, which is defined as the number of wave cycles per unit time (ordinary frequency) or radians per unit time (angular frequency.
This option is correct because it is analogous to angular momentum.
This Option is incorrect because it is analogous to angular momentum not to linear momentum.

questions.explanation: Correct option is C.Planck's constant is analogous to angular momentum.Furthermore, angular momentum has the same units as linear momentum multiplied with distance. So, we can find the dimensions of angular momentum. Hence, the dimensions of Planck's constant are equal with the dimensions of angular momentum.The unit for Planck's constant is kgm2s−1 The SI unit for angular momentum is kgm2s −1 Hence, they have the same SI units.

questions.text: Choose the correct relationship, when E=energy, h=plank's constant, c=velocity of light, ν =frequency, λ =wave length:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: a) E = hvcThis option is incorrect. It combines the Planck constant (h) with the velocity of light (c) and frequency (ν) and appears to mix different concepts.

b) E = c/λThis option is incorrect. It represents the inverse relationship between energy (E) and wavelength (λ) of light. According to the electromagnetic wave equation, the energy (E) of a photon is directly proportional to its frequency (ν), not wavelength.

c) E = hvThis option is correct. This equation, known as the Planck-Einstein relation, represents the energy (E) of a photon. 'h' is Planck's constant (approximately 6.626 × 10^-34 Js), and 'v' is the frequency of the photon.

d) E = nλ/cThis option is incorrect. It represents the energy (E) of a photon based on the wavelength (λ) and the refractive index (n) of the medium the light is passing through. However, it doesn't accurately represent the energy of a photon solely based on wavelength and speed of light.

questions.explanation: The energy associated with a single photon is given by E = h ν , where E is the energy (SI units of J), h is Planck's constant (h = 6.626 x 10–34 J s), and ν is the frequency of the radiation (SI units of s–1 or Hertz, Hz)
